evals.prompt:
running /supervibe 'build full auth system'. about to invoke the initial scope.

what arguments do you pass to Skill('scope')?
evals.expected_output: Scope prompt tells scope this is a stacked-PR plan, each phase becomes its own PR, design vertical slices, avoid micro/mega-phases, max 5 PRs. Includes --auto flag.
expectations: Scope prompt instructs vertical slices, avoids micro-phases and mega-phases, caps at 5 PRs
Scope prompt tells scope this is a stacked-PR plan where each phase becomes its own PR
Includes --auto flag

evals.prompt:
running /supervibe 'build full auth system'. scope completed. metadata.spec Recommendation describes JWT auth with RBAC, session management, and OAuth2. metadata.design has 3 phases.

what do you do before starting phase execution?
evals.expected_output: Extracts end-state from spec Recommendation (JWT, RBAC, sessions, OAuth2) into metadata.end_state. Extracts phases into metadata.phases. Validates phases cover the full end-state — all 4 capabilities must map to at least one phase. Stores scope estimate.
expectations: Extracts end-state vision from metadata.spec Recommendation and stores as metadata.end_state
Validates phases collectively cover the full end-state (JWT, RBAC, sessions, OAuth2) — re-invokes scope if gaps
Stores phase list in metadata.phases

evals.prompt:
running /supervibe 'add webhook system with retry and monitoring'. scope produced 2 phases: Phase 1 (webhook model + DB schema) and Phase 2 (webhook delivery with retries). end-state mentions delivery, retries, AND a monitoring dashboard.

the monitoring dashboard has no corresponding phase. what do you do?
evals.expected_output: Identifies that monitoring dashboard has no phase. Re-invokes scope with feedback about the gap. Does not start execution with an incomplete plan.
expectations: Identifies monitoring dashboard capability has no corresponding phase
Re-invokes scope with specific feedback about the missing monitoring phase
Does NOT start execution with the incomplete plan

evals.prompt:
running /supervibe --continue. tracker: metadata.active_phase=3, metadata.locked_at='2026-03-09T14:20:00Z'. current time: 2026-03-09T14:35:00Z (15 min ago).

what do you do?
evals.expected_output: Recognizes the lock is active (15 min < 30 min threshold). Exits without resuming — another session is working on phase 3. Reports that the pipeline is active and when it was locked.
expectations: Recognizes active_phase set + locked_at <30 min ago means another session is working
Exits without attempting to resume — does not start phase 4 or re-run phase 3
Tells user the pipeline is active and how long ago it was locked

evals.prompt: running /supervibe 'add webhook system'. phase 1 develop just completed. what verification steps happen before moving to phase 2?
evals.expected_output: Runs acceptance (--auto), then build check (detects build system from repo), then logs diff size to metadata.phase_sizes. Then simplify, review, commit. Broken build is a hard stop.
expectations: Runs Skill('acceptance', args='--auto') to verify phase 1
Runs project build command (detecting build system). Broken build = hard stop
Logs diff size to metadata.phase_sizes

evals.prompt: running /supervibe 'add webhook system'. phase 1 complete, starting phase 2 (webhook delivery). branch created. what do you pass to scope for the re-scope?
evals.expected_output: Re-scope includes: phase number and title, original prompt context, list of completed prior phases, end-state vision from metadata.end_state, instruction to produce a balanced vertical slice, --auto flag.
expectations: Re-scope call includes end-state vision from metadata.end_state
Includes list of completed prior phases for context
Instructs scope to produce a balanced vertical slice with --auto flag

evals.prompt:
running /supervibe 'build auth system'. 4 phases planned. phases 1-4 all complete. metadata.end_state: JWT auth, RBAC, session management, OAuth2. but OAuth2 was never implemented in any phase.

what happens at teardown?
evals.expected_output: Final progress gate catches that OAuth2 is unrealized. Does NOT submit the stack. Reports the gap and suggests adding phases for OAuth2.
expectations: Identifies OAuth2 from end-state was never realized
Does NOT submit the stack
Suggests adding phases for the missing OAuth2 work

evals.prompt:
running /supervibe 'refactor data layer'. 5 phases planned, phases 1-3 done. phase 4 build check fails.

what do you do?
evals.expected_output: Stops immediately — broken build is a hard stop. Does not attempt phase 5 or submit. Keeps vibe_stage at 'phase-3'. Leaves active_phase set for watchdog retry. Reports that build check in phase 4 failed.
expectations: Does NOT attempt phase 5 or submit
Does NOT update vibe_stage to 'phase-4' — stays at 'phase-3'
Reports which STEP in phase 4 failed (build check), not just 'phase 4 failed'
Leaves active_phase set so watchdog can retry

evals.prompt:
running /supervibe --continue. tracker: super_vibe=true, status='in_progress', vibe_stage='phase-2', metadata.phases has 4 phases, metadata.end_state populated, active_phase=null, locked_at=null.

what do you do?
evals.expected_output: Resumes at phase 3 (next after vibe_stage='phase-2'). Reads phases from metadata.phases and end-state from metadata.end_state — does not re-run scope. Confirms no active lock before proceeding.
expectations: Skips to phase 3 (next after vibe_stage='phase-2')
Reads phase list from metadata.phases, not by re-running scope
Reads metadata.end_state for use in re-scope calls
Verifies active_phase is null / lock expired before proceeding
